pack_name: Law and Legal Sector Compliance Overlay
description: Sector overlay for South African law firms, legal practitioners, legal services documentation, client engagements, professional conduct, confidentiality, AML/CFT, and personal-information handling.

metadata.scope: South Africa-only sector overlay controls for Law and Legal Sector Compliance Overlay. Intended to be used with south_africa_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official South African legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Legal Practice Act 28 of 2014
primary_sources.issuer: Republic of South Africa / Legal Practice Council

primary_sources.name: Code of Conduct for Legal Practitioners
Financial Intelligence Centre Act, 2001 and FIC compliance guidance
Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013 (POPIA)
primary_sources.issuer: Information Regulator / Republic of South Africa

primary_sources.name: Promotion of Access to Information Act, 2000 (PAIA)
primary_sources.issuer: Republic of South Africa / Information Regulator

rules.title: Client confidentiality, privilege, or file-confidentiality controls are documented
rules.summary: Checks for client confidentiality, legal professional privilege, privileged communication, file confidentiality, or conflict-sensitive handling evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Legal Practice Council admission, enrolment, or practice-authority evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for Legal Practice Council, enrolment, admission, fidelity fund certificate, law firm, or practice number evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Professional conduct, mandate, fee, or client-care evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for mandate/engagement letter, fee disclosure, client-care terms, professional conduct, or complaints process evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Legal-practitioner AML/CFT RMCP and CDD controls are present where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for FIC registration, RMCP, client identification, beneficial ownership, sanctions screening, or suspicious transaction evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: FIC Act obligations for legal practitioners

rules.title: Client personal-information protection and access-to-information controls are present
rules.summary: Checks for POPIA, personal information, information officer, data subject rights, PAIA, or access request evidence.
